item.title: Opendoor’s India exit is fueling a bigger conversation about AI and outsourcing
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The decision comes as India emerges as the world’s largest GCC market.

item.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: If you doubted his genius, doubt no more.

item.title: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX, alleging he was fired for raising AI safety concerns about Grok days before SpaceX's historic IPO.

item.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Companies are burning through exorbitant sums of money to keep pace in the AI arms race. Debt is climbing.

item.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

item.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

item.title: Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.

item.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.

item.title: The three hard-tech moonshots fueling SpaceX’s unbelievable IPO
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.

item.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Through the acquisition, WMG aims to better track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content or for training AI models.

item.title: Jedify raises $24M to help companies arm AI agents with context on their business
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The funding round was led by Norwest, with participation from S Capital VC, Cerca Partners, and Oceans Ventures. Snowflake Ventures also participated as a strategic investor.

item.title: Decart’s new world model can simulate hours of photorealistic driving — with some caveats
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Decart is launching Oasis 3, a real-time world model that generates photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing, now available via API for developers to build on.

item.title: Meta signs first AI data center deal in India with Reliance
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The 168-megawatt facility will support Meta's global AI computing needs and can be expanded over time.

item.title: Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek, Connie Loizos
item.description: Google just made it significantly cheaper to enjoy its budget AI subscription tier.

item.title: How Justin Ernest invested nearly $500M into hot startups without a traditional VC fund
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest in startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.

item.title: Hey, Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

item.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is going to be a big hit with the web's vibe coders.

item.title: Can tech companies learn to love cheaper AI models?
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: If those same AI workloads can be handled by cheaper models without affecting quality, it would mean a massive shift in the economics of AI.

item.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more
item.dc:creator: Morgan Little, Aisha Malik
item.description: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its long-standing Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.
